description.overview: Akitas are large, powerful dogs from Japan known for their dignity and fierce loyalty. They're symbols of good health, happiness, and long life in Japan.
description.temperament: Akitas are loyal and protective, devoted to family but wary of strangers. They can be aggressive toward other dogs, especially same sex.
description.health: Prone to hip dysplasia, autoimmune disorders, and bloat. Regular vet checkups important.
description.exercise: Moderate exercise needs. Daily activity required. Mental stimulation important.

best_for: People who can provide firm leadership
verdict.summary: Dignified, fiercely loyal protectors best for experienced owners. Akitas are devoted to family but wary of strangers and may be aggressive with other dogs. Need confident leadership and early socialization.

description.overview: The Alaskan Malamute is one of the oldest Arctic sled dogs, originally bred for hauling heavy freight. They're powerful, substantial dogs with a deep chest and strong, well-muscled body. Despite their wolf-like appearance, they're friendly and affectionate with people.
description.history: Developed by the Mahlemut Inuit people of Alaska's Norton Sound region, Malamutes were essential for survival in the harsh Arctic environment. They pulled heavy sleds, hunted seals, and protected their families from polar bears. Unlike some breeds created for speed, Malamutes were bred for strength and endurance.
description.temperament: Malamutes are friendly and outgoing with people, including strangers—they make poor guard dogs. They're loyal and devoted to their families but have an independent streak. They can be challenging to train due to their stubbornness, but they're highly intelligent. They may be aggressive toward other dogs, especially same-sex dogs.
description.health: Generally healthy breed. Watch for hip dysplasia, cataracts, chondrodysplasia (dwarfism), hypothyroidism, and inherited polyneuropathy. Regular vet checkups and health screenings are recommended.
description.grooming: Heavy shedders, especially twice a year during 'coat blow' season. Regular brushing (2-3 times per week, daily during shedding season) is essential. They're relatively clean dogs and typically don't have a strong odor.
description.exercise: High exercise needs—at least 2 hours daily. They excel at hiking, backpacking, sledding, and weight pulling. Without adequate exercise, they can become destructive. They love cold weather and may struggle in hot climates.

best_for: People who enjoy outdoor activities (hiking, camping)
not_for: Homes with small pets (high prey drive)
verdict.summary: The Alaskan Malamute is perfect for active, experienced dog owners who love the outdoors and can provide firm, consistent leadership. They need space to run, plenty of exercise, and thrive in cold climates. Not recommended for apartments, hot climates, or first-time owners.

description.overview: Australian Cattle Dogs are tough, compact herding dogs bred to drive cattle across rough terrain. They're incredibly intelligent and hardworking.
description.temperament: ACDs are loyal, protective, and wary of strangers. They're devoted to their owners and need jobs to do. May nip at heels (herding instinct).
description.health: Generally very healthy and long-lived. Watch for hip dysplasia, deafness, and eye problems.
description.exercise: Very high exercise needs. Need vigorous daily activity and mental challenges. Excel at herding and dog sports.

verdict.summary: Tough, intelligent working dogs that need extensive exercise and mental challenges. ACDs are loyal and protective but need experienced owners. Best for active rural/suburban living with jobs to do.

description.overview: Despite the name, Australian Shepherds were developed in the American West. They're intelligent, work-oriented dogs with beautiful coats and often striking blue or multicolored eyes.
description.temperament: Aussies are extremely smart and eager to please. They need jobs to do or they'll create their own. May try to herd children and pets.
description.health: Prone to hip dysplasia, epilepsy, and eye problems. Merle-to-merle breeding can cause deafness and blindness.
description.exercise: Very high exercise and mental stimulation needs. Excel at agility, herding trials, and dog sports.

verdict.summary: Brilliant, athletic dogs that need extensive exercise and mental challenges. Perfect for active owners interested in dog sports. Will become destructive if bored.

description.overview: Basset Hounds are low-slung, long-eared dogs with incredible noses. They're laid-back companions with stubborn streaks and distinctive bays.
description.temperament: Bassets are patient, good-natured, and friendly with everyone. They can be stubborn and will follow their noses anywhere.
description.health: Prone to obesity, ear infections, back problems, and bloat. Keep them lean and clean their ears regularly.
description.exercise: Low to moderate exercise needs. Daily walks but at their own pace. Watch their weight.

best_for: People who enjoy a unique personality
verdict.summary: Lovably stubborn couch companions. Basset Hounds are patient and friendly but follow their noses and can be loud. Best for patient owners who appreciate their unique charm.

description.overview: Small, compact, and hardy, Beagles are active companions for kids and adults alike. Their convenient size, adaptable nature, and joyful personality make them one of the most popular family dogs.
description.history: Ancient breed developed in England for hunting rabbits. Their excellent sense of smell made them valuable hunting companions. Today they're also used as detection dogs at airports.
description.temperament: Beagles are happy-go-lucky and friendly. They love company and don't like being left alone. They can be vocal and have a distinctive bay.
description.health: Generally healthy but prone to epilepsy, hypothyroidism, hip dysplasia, and eye conditions. Watch their weight—they love food.
description.grooming: Easy to maintain. Weekly brushing and occasional baths. Regular ear cleaning is important due to their floppy ears.
description.exercise: Moderate to high exercise needs. Need daily walks and playtime. Their nose can lead them astray—always use a leash or fenced area.

verdict.summary: Beagles are friendly, curious, and great with kids. Perfect for active families who don't mind some barking. They hate being alone and will follow their nose anywhere. Best with company and secure fencing.

coat_colors: fawn to mahogany with black mask and ears

description.overview: Belgian Malinois are elite working dogs used by military and police worldwide. They're intelligent, driven, and need experienced handling.
description.temperament: Malinois are confident, protective, and extremely work-oriented. They bond closely with handlers and need constant mental and physical challenges.
description.health: Generally healthy and athletic. Watch for hip/elbow dysplasia and eye problems.
description.exercise: Extremely high exercise and work needs. Need jobs to do. Not pets for casual owners.

best_for: Those who can provide full-time engagement
verdict.summary: Elite working dogs—NOT family pets for average owners. Malinois need professional-level handling, extensive exercise, and constant work. Only for experienced handlers who understand working dogs.

coat_colors: tricolor: black with white and rust

description.overview: Bernese Mountain Dogs are big, beautiful, and gentle. Originally farm dogs in Switzerland, they're now beloved family companions known for their calm demeanor.
description.temperament: Berners are good-natured and calm. They're devoted to family and gentle with children. Can be reserved with strangers but not aggressive.
description.health: Unfortunately prone to cancer and have shorter lifespans. Also watch for hip/elbow dysplasia and bloat.
description.exercise: Moderate exercise needs. Daily walks and activities. Enjoy pulling carts and drafting work.

verdict.summary: Beautiful, gentle family dogs with unfortunately short lifespans. Berners are wonderful with children and devoted to family. Need space and shed heavily. Best for those prepared for potential health issues.

description.overview: Bichon Frises are cheerful, small dogs with powder-puff appearances. They're playful, gentle companions that love everyone they meet.
description.temperament: Bichons are happy, playful, and gentle. They love people and get along with everyone—kids, strangers, and other pets.
description.health: Generally healthy. Watch for allergies, bladder issues, luxating patella, and dental problems.
description.exercise: Moderate exercise needs. Daily walks and play sessions. Good for apartments.

verdict.summary: Cheerful, hypoallergenic companions perfect for families and apartments. Bichons need regular grooming but reward you with endless affection. Great for first-time owners and allergy sufferers.

description.overview: Border Collies are considered the most intelligent dog breed. They were developed for herding sheep and have incredible work ethic and energy.
description.temperament: Border Collies are intensely focused and work-driven. They need mental and physical challenges. May try to herd children and pets.
description.health: Generally healthy but prone to hip dysplasia, epilepsy, and eye problems. CEA testing recommended.
description.exercise: Extremely high exercise and mental stimulation needs. Need jobs to do. Excel at all dog sports.

best_for: Those who can provide mental challenges
verdict.summary: The Einstein of dogs. Border Collies are brilliant but demanding—they need hours of activity and mental stimulation daily. Best for experienced, active owners. Will become destructive if bored.

description.overview: Boston Terriers are compact, friendly dogs with tuxedo-like markings. Known as the 'American Gentleman,' they're charming and adaptable companions.
description.temperament: Bostons are friendly and adaptable. They get along with everyone—people, kids, and other pets. Lively but not hyperactive.
description.health: Brachycephalic breed prone to breathing issues, eye problems, and overheating. Choose breeders who prioritize health.
description.exercise: Moderate exercise needs. Short walks and play sessions. Avoid strenuous activity in heat.

verdict.summary: The perfect city dog. Boston Terriers are friendly, low-maintenance, and adaptable to apartment living. Watch for breathing issues common to flat-faced breeds. Great for first-time owners.

description.overview: Boxers are bright, fun-loving, and active dogs. They're patient with children and make excellent family dogs. Their expressive faces and playful nature make them irresistible.
description.temperament: Boxers are patient, protective, and playful. They mature slowly and maintain puppy-like energy well into adulthood. Great with kids and loyal to family.
description.health: Prone to cancer, heart conditions, hip dysplasia, and bloat. They can overheat easily. Regular vet checkups important.
description.exercise: High exercise needs. At least 2 hours daily of activity. They need mental stimulation too.

verdict.summary: Boxers are playful, patient family dogs that stay puppy-like for years. Great with kids but need lots of exercise. Watch for health issues common to the breed.

description.overview: Brittanys are versatile hunting dogs and excellent family companions. They're energetic, friendly, and eager to please.
description.temperament: Brittanys are bright, eager, and sensitive. They respond well to positive training and love being with their families.
description.health: Generally healthy. Watch for hip dysplasia and epilepsy. Regular vet checkups recommended.
description.exercise: Very high exercise needs. Need vigorous daily activity. Excel at field trials and hunt tests.

not_for: First-time owners unprepared for high energy
verdict.summary: High-energy hunting dogs that need lots of exercise and activity. Brittanys are sweet, eager to please, and great family dogs—if you can keep up with them.

description.overview: Bull Terriers are unique dogs with egg-shaped heads and playful personalities. They're muscular, mischievous, and full of personality.
description.temperament: Bull Terriers are playful, charming, and mischievous. They're devoted to their families and can be clownish. Need firm, consistent training.
description.health: Prone to deafness (especially white dogs), heart problems, skin allergies, and kidney issues.
description.exercise: High exercise needs. Need vigorous daily activity and mental stimulation to prevent destructive behavior.

best_for: Those who enjoy a unique-looking dog
not_for: Families with small children or pets
verdict.summary: Unique, mischievous clowns that need experienced owners. Bull Terriers are devoted and entertaining but require firm training and lots of exercise. Best for active owners who appreciate their quirky personalities.

description.overview: The Bulldog is a thick-set, low-slung, well-muscled dog with a wrinkled face and a distinctive pushed-in nose. Despite their grumpy expression, Bulldogs are gentle and make excellent family companions.
description.history: Originally bred for bull-baiting in England. When the sport was banned in 1835, breeders worked to transform the aggressive fighter into a gentle companion.
description.temperament: Bulldogs are calm, courageous, and friendly. They form strong bonds with children and are known for their patience. They can be stubborn but respond well to patient training.
description.health: Prone to many health issues including breathing problems, hip dysplasia, skin infections, and overheating. Regular vet care and choosing healthy bloodlines is essential.
description.grooming: Weekly brushing. Daily wrinkle cleaning to prevent infections. Keep facial folds dry and clean.
description.exercise: Low exercise needs. Short walks in cool weather are best. Prone to overheating—avoid exercise in hot weather.

best_for: First-time owners wanting a mellow pet
verdict.summary: Bulldogs are gentle, calm companions perfect for relaxed households. Great with kids and apartment-friendly. However, expect significant health issues and vet bills. Best for those who can handle their special health needs.

description.overview: Cavaliers are the ideal companion dogs. They combine the gentleness of a toy breed with the athleticism of a spaniel. Sweet-natured and adaptable.
description.temperament: Cavaliers are affectionate and adaptable. They love everyone—people, dogs, even cats. They're equally happy on a lap or on a hike.
description.health: Prone to heart disease (mitral valve disease) and syringomyelia. Buy only from health-tested breeders. Regular cardiac screening important.
description.exercise: Moderate exercise needs. Adaptable to owner's lifestyle. Happy with walks or more active pursuits.

verdict.summary: The ideal companion dog—adaptable, affectionate, and gentle. Great for families, seniors, and apartments. Be aware of serious health issues in the breed and buy from health-tested breeders.

description.overview: Chihuahuas are the smallest dog breed but have huge personalities. They're loyal to their owners and can be feisty despite their tiny size.
description.temperament: Chihuahuas are loyal and devoted to their owners. They can be suspicious of strangers and may not tolerate rough handling.
description.health: Prone to heart problems, luxating patella, hypoglycemia, and dental issues. Handle with care.
description.exercise: Low exercise needs. Short walks and indoor play. Sensitive to cold.

best_for: Those wanting a loyal tiny companion
verdict.summary: Tiny but mighty. Chihuahuas are loyal and devoted to their owners but can be suspicious of others. Best for adults in apartments. Not ideal for families with young children.

description.overview: Cocker Spaniels are happy, gentle dogs with beautiful long ears and silky coats. They're excellent family companions and the smallest of the sporting breeds.
description.temperament: Cockers are happy and gentle. They love people and are eager to please. Generally good with children and other pets.
description.health: Prone to ear infections, eye problems, and hip dysplasia. Keep ears clean and dry.
description.exercise: Moderate exercise needs. Daily walks and playtime. Enjoy activities but adaptable to calmer lifestyles.

verdict.summary: Happy, gentle family dogs with beautiful coats. Cockers need regular grooming but reward you with loyal, loving companionship. Great for families and first-time owners.

description.overview: Collies are the graceful, devoted dogs immortalized by Lassie. They're intelligent, loyal family companions known for their devotion to children.
description.temperament: Collies are gentle, devoted, and highly attuned to their family's emotions. They're excellent with children and protective without aggression.
description.health: Prone to eye problems (CEA), MDR1 gene (drug sensitivity), and hip dysplasia. Get eyes and MDR1 tested.
description.exercise: Moderate exercise needs. Daily walks and play. Enjoy herding activities and dog sports.

not_for: Those who dislike barking or grooming
verdict.summary: The ultimate family dog—loyal, gentle, and wonderful with children. Collies need regular grooming and space but reward you with unwavering devotion. Perfect for families.

description.overview: The iconic 'wiener dog' is famously long and low. Curious, lively, and charming with a big-dog bark, Dachshunds are clever and courageous, sometimes to the point of rashness.
description.history: Developed in Germany to hunt badgers—'dachshund' means 'badger dog.' Their unique shape allowed them to dig into burrows and confront prey underground.
description.temperament: Dachshunds are bold and curious with a stubborn streak. They bond closely with their owners but can be suspicious of strangers. They have a surprisingly loud bark.
description.health: Prone to back problems (IVDD) due to their long spine. Also watch for obesity, dental issues, and eye conditions. Avoid jumping and stairs.
description.grooming: Depends on coat type. Smooth coats need minimal care. Wirehaired need stripping. Longhaired need regular brushing.
description.exercise: Moderate exercise needs. Daily walks but avoid activities that strain the back. Keep them lean to protect their spine.

best_for: Those wanting a small but bold dog
People who enjoy a dog with personality
verdict.summary: Dachshunds pack huge personality into a small, unique package. Great for apartments and city living. They can be stubborn and prone to back problems. Best for owners who appreciate their independent spirit.

description.overview: Dobermans are sleek, powerful, and among the most intelligent breeds. They're loyal protectors and excellent family dogs when properly trained and socialized.
description.temperament: Dobermans are loyal, fearless, and highly trainable. They bond closely with their families and are natural protectors. Need early socialization.
description.health: Prone to dilated cardiomyopathy, von Willebrand's disease, hip dysplasia, and wobbler syndrome. Regular cardiac screening important.
description.exercise: High exercise needs. Need daily vigorous activity and mental stimulation. Excel at obedience and protection sports.

not_for: Those who can't commit to training
verdict.summary: Elegant, intelligent, and fiercely loyal. Dobermans need experienced owners who can provide training, exercise, and socialization. Excellent family protectors when raised right.

description.overview: English Springer Spaniels are friendly, athletic dogs bred to 'spring' game for hunters. They're excellent family companions and versatile sporting dogs.
description.temperament: Springers are friendly, eager to please, and playful. They love people and are great with children. Active and need engagement.
description.health: Generally healthy but prone to hip dysplasia, eye problems, and ear infections. Clean ears regularly.
description.exercise: High exercise needs. Daily vigorous activity, swimming, and field work.

verdict.summary: Friendly, athletic family dogs that love children and activities. Springers need regular exercise and engagement. Great for active families who want a loving, playful companion.

description.overview: The French Bulldog has enjoyed a surge in popularity. The adaptable Frenchie is the ideal companion for city dwellers—they don't need a lot of space and are content with short walks and indoor play.
description.history: Descended from English Bulldogs brought to France by lace workers. French breeders developed the distinctive bat ears and compact size that define the breed today.
description.temperament: Frenchies are charming, adaptable, and make excellent companions. They're playful but not overly energetic. They love human contact and get along well with other pets.
description.health: Brachycephalic breed prone to breathing issues, overheating, spinal disorders, and eye problems. Choose breeders who prioritize health over extreme features.
description.grooming: Easy to groom—weekly brushing and regular facial fold cleaning. Keep wrinkles dry to prevent infections.
description.exercise: Low to moderate exercise needs. Short walks and indoor play are sufficient. Avoid strenuous exercise, especially in hot weather.

not_for: Families wanting a very active dog
verdict.summary: The perfect apartment dog. French Bulldogs are affectionate, adaptable, and don't need much exercise. Be prepared for potential health issues and vet costs. Ideal for city dwellers and those wanting a charming, low-maintenance companion.

description.overview: German Shepherds are working dogs developed for herding sheep. Because of their strength, intelligence, trainability, and obedience, they're often preferred for many types of work including police, military, and search-and-rescue.
description.history: Developed in Germany in the late 1800s by Captain Max von Stephanitz, who wanted to create the ideal herding dog. The breed quickly proved versatile and became popular worldwide for various working roles.
description.temperament: GSDs are confident, courageous, and smart. They're willing to put their life on the line for loved ones. They can be aloof with strangers but are loyal and loving with family.
description.health: Prone to hip and elbow dysplasia, degenerative myelopathy, and bloat. Regular vet checkups are important. Buy from reputable breeders who test for genetic conditions.
description.grooming: Heavy shedders—brush several times a week. They 'blow' their coat twice a year when daily brushing is needed. Regular nail, ear, and dental care.
description.exercise: High exercise needs. At least 2 hours daily of physical and mental stimulation. Excel at obedience, agility, tracking, and protection sports.

verdict.summary: The German Shepherd is a loyal, intelligent, and versatile working dog. Best suited for experienced owners who can provide firm leadership, consistent training, and plenty of exercise. Not ideal for first-time owners or apartment living.

description.overview: The German Shorthaired Pointer is a versatile hunting dog and all-purpose gun dog. They're known for their power, speed, agility, and endurance—plus a striking appearance.
description.history: Developed in 19th century Germany by crossing various breeds to create the ideal versatile hunting dog that could point, retrieve, and track on both land and water.
description.temperament: GSPs are friendly, smart, and enthusiastic. They're eager to please and bond closely with their family. They can be boisterous, especially when young.
description.health: Generally healthy but prone to hip dysplasia, bloat, and some eye conditions. Responsible breeding is important.
description.grooming: Very easy to groom. Weekly brushing and occasional baths. Low maintenance coat.
description.exercise: Very high exercise needs—at least 2 hours daily. Need vigorous activity. Not suited for sedentary lifestyles.

verdict.summary: The ultimate athlete. GSPs need extensive daily exercise and are perfect for active owners who run, hike, or hunt. They're friendly and trainable but will become destructive without enough activity. Not for couch potatoes.

description.overview: The Golden Retriever is one of the most popular dog breeds. Their friendly, tolerant attitude makes them fabulous family pets, and their intelligence makes them highly capable working dogs.
description.history: Developed in Scotland during the mid-19th century by Lord Tweedmouth, who crossed a yellow retriever with a Tweed Water Spaniel to create the perfect gundog for retrieving waterfowl.
description.temperament: Goldens are outgoing, trustworthy, and eager to please. They're wonderful with children and other pets. Their patient, gentle nature makes them excellent therapy dogs.
description.health: Prone to cancer, hip dysplasia, elbow dysplasia, and heart conditions. Regular vet visits and buying from health-tested breeders is important.
description.grooming: Heavy shedders requiring regular brushing (daily during shedding season). Their feathered coat needs attention to prevent matting.
description.exercise: Active dogs needing 1-2 hours of exercise daily. Love swimming, fetch, and any activity with their family. Excel at obedience and agility.

verdict.summary: The Golden Retriever is the quintessential family dog—friendly, patient, and eager to please. Perfect for first-time owners and families. They need regular exercise and grooming but reward you with unwavering loyalty and love.

description.overview: Great Danes are gentle giants. Despite their imposing size, they're affectionate, patient, and make wonderful family companions. They think they're lap dogs.
description.temperament: Danes are friendly and patient. They're great with children and other pets. Despite their size, they're often called 'gentle giants.'
description.health: Shorter lifespan due to size. Prone to bloat, heart disease, hip dysplasia, and bone cancer. Bloat is a medical emergency.
description.exercise: Moderate exercise needs. Daily walks but avoid strenuous exercise while growing. Joint-friendly activities preferred.

best_for: People who can handle large dogs
verdict.summary: Gentle giants with hearts as big as their bodies. Great Danes are patient and loving but have shorter lifespans and high costs. Best for those with space and budget for a giant breed.

description.overview: The Havanese is Cuba's national dog. They're cheerful, intelligent, and make wonderful companions. Known for their springy gait and silky coat.
description.temperament: Havanese are happy, outgoing dogs who love everyone. They're intelligent and eager to please. Great with children and other pets.
description.health: Generally healthy but watch for eye problems, luxating patella, and hip dysplasia.
description.exercise: Moderate exercise needs. Playful and enjoy activities with their owners. Good for apartment living.

verdict.summary: Cheerful, intelligent, and hypoallergenic. Havanese are wonderful family dogs that do well in apartments. Need regular grooming but don't shed much. Great first-time dog.

description.overview: The Labrador Retriever is America's most popular dog breed for good reason. They're friendly, outgoing, and high-spirited companions who have more than enough affection to go around for a family looking for a medium-to-large dog.
description.history: Despite the name, Labrador Retrievers actually originated in Newfoundland, Canada, where they helped fishermen retrieve nets and catch escaping fish. British nobles visiting Canada fell in love with the breed and brought them to England.
description.temperament: Labs are famously friendly. They are companionable housemates who bond with the whole family, and they socialize well with neighbor dogs and humans alike. But don't mistake their easygoing personality for low energy.
description.health: Generally healthy, but prone to hip and elbow dysplasia, heart disorders, eye conditions, and exercise-induced collapse. Obesity is common, so watch their diet.
description.grooming: Weekly brushing keeps the coat healthy. They shed heavily, especially during seasonal changes. Regular nail trimming and ear cleaning needed.
description.exercise: High energy dogs that need lots of exercise—at least an hour daily. They love swimming, fetch, and any activity with their humans.

verdict.summary: The ultimate family dog. Labrador Retrievers are friendly, patient, and trainable—perfect for first-time owners and families with kids. They need plenty of exercise and shed a lot, but their loving nature makes them worth it.

description.overview: Maltese are elegant toy dogs with flowing white coats. They've been lap dogs of royalty for centuries and remain popular companions today.
description.temperament: Maltese are gentle and affectionate but surprisingly fearless. They love their people and enjoy being pampered.
description.health: Prone to dental issues, luxating patella, liver shunts, and collapsed trachea. Regular dental care essential.
description.exercise: Low to moderate exercise needs. Short walks and indoor play. Good for apartments.

best_for: Those wanting a glamorous small dog
verdict.summary: Elegant, affectionate lap dogs perfect for apartment living. Maltese need daily grooming but are hypoallergenic. Best for adults who can handle them gently and enjoy pampering.

description.overview: Mini American Shepherds are smaller versions of Australian Shepherds—smart, athletic, and versatile. Perfect for those who want Aussie traits in a smaller package.
description.temperament: MAS are intelligent, loyal, and eager to please. They bond closely with their families and need mental stimulation.
description.health: Generally healthy. Watch for eye problems and hip dysplasia. MDR1 gene testing recommended.
description.exercise: High exercise and mental stimulation needs. Excel at agility and other dog sports.

best_for: Those wanting a smart, portable companion
not_for: Those without time for exercise and training
First-time owners unprepared for high energy
verdict.summary: All the brains and drive of an Aussie in a portable package. MAS need lots of exercise and mental challenges. Perfect for active owners who want a trainable, devoted smaller companion.

description.overview: Miniature Schnauzers are friendly, intelligent dogs with distinctive beards. They're the most popular of the three Schnauzer sizes and make excellent family companions.
description.temperament: Friendly and obedient but with terrier spirit. They're alert watchdogs with a tendency to bark. Good with children and other dogs.
description.health: Generally healthy but prone to pancreatitis, diabetes, and eye problems. Keep them on a healthy diet.
description.exercise: Moderate exercise needs. Daily walks and play sessions. Enjoy dog sports and training.

not_for: Those who prefer very calm dogs
verdict.summary: Friendly, smart, and hypoallergenic. Mini Schnauzers make great apartment dogs and family companions. They need regular grooming and can be barky but are loyal and fun.

description.overview: Newfoundlands are gentle giants famous for water rescues. They're sweet-tempered, devoted dogs that are exceptional with children.
description.temperament: Newfies are sweet, patient, and devoted. They're natural guardians of children and known for their calm, gentle nature.
description.health: Prone to hip/elbow dysplasia, heart conditions, and bloat. Shorter lifespan due to size. Regular cardiac screening important.
description.exercise: Moderate exercise needs. Enjoy swimming. Avoid strenuous activity in heat—they overheat easily.

best_for: Patient owners who can handle drool and hair
verdict.summary: The ultimate nanny dog—Newfies are sweet, gentle giants that adore children. They need space, tolerate drool and shedding, and have shorter lifespans. Perfect for families who want a devoted gentle giant.

description.overview: The Pembroke Welsh Corgi is a strong, athletic little dog. They're one of the world's most popular herding breeds and have been favored by British royalty for decades.
description.history: Brought to Wales by Flemish weavers around 1100 AD. Bred to herd cattle by nipping at their heels—their low stature helped them avoid kicks.
description.temperament: Corgis are smart, affectionate, and surprisingly athletic. They have big-dog personalities in compact bodies. Can be vocal and may try to herd children.
description.health: Prone to hip dysplasia, eye problems, and degenerative myelopathy. Keep them lean—obesity worsens joint problems.
description.grooming: Heavy shedders requiring regular brushing. They 'blow' their coat seasonally. Weekly brushing minimum, daily during shedding.
description.exercise: Moderate to high exercise needs despite size. Daily walks and play sessions. They're more athletic than they look.

best_for: Those wanting a small but sturdy dog
verdict.summary: Corgis are smart, athletic, and full of personality. Perfect for active owners who want a small but capable dog. Heavy shedders and can be vocal. Great for families with older children.

description.overview: Pomeranians are tiny dogs with big personalities. They're lively, bold, and full of energy despite their small size. Famous for their fluffy coats and foxy faces.
description.temperament: Poms are bold and lively. They often don't realize they're small and may challenge larger dogs. Loyal to their owners but can be suspicious of strangers.
description.health: Prone to luxating patella, collapsed trachea, dental issues, and hair loss. Handle gently due to small size.
description.exercise: Low to moderate exercise needs. Short walks and indoor play. Good for apartments.

best_for: Those wanting a bold tiny companion
verdict.summary: Big personality in a tiny, fluffy package. Pomeranians are bold and lively but can be barky. Great for apartments and adults. Not ideal for families with young children due to their delicate size.

description.overview: Poodles are among the most intelligent dog breeds. They come in three sizes (Standard, Miniature, Toy) and are known for their curly, hypoallergenic coat and elegant appearance.
description.history: Originally bred in Germany as water retrievers. The breed became associated with France where it was refined and became the national dog. The elaborate clips were functional—designed to protect joints and organs in cold water.
description.temperament: Poodles are extremely smart, active, and excel at obedience training. They're eager to please and good with families. They can be reserved with strangers but aren't aggressive.
description.health: Generally healthy but prone to hip dysplasia, epilepsy, progressive retinal atrophy, and Addison's disease. Regular health screenings recommended.
description.grooming: High maintenance coat requiring professional grooming every 4-6 weeks. Daily brushing to prevent matting. The trade-off is minimal shedding.
description.exercise: Active dogs needing daily exercise. Standards need more than Toys. They excel at agility, obedience, and retriever activities.

best_for: Those wanting a highly trainable dog
verdict.summary: Poodles are intelligent, elegant, and hypoallergenic—perfect for allergy sufferers. Available in three sizes to suit different lifestyles. High grooming needs but minimal shedding. Excellent for training enthusiasts and active families.

description.overview: Portuguese Water Dogs are athletic, intelligent dogs bred to help fishermen. They're energetic, love water, and became famous as the Obama family dogs.
description.temperament: Porties are affectionate, smart, and eager to please. They love water and need plenty of activity. Bond closely with families.
description.health: Generally healthy. Watch for hip dysplasia, eye problems, and GM1 storage disease. Get health testing.
description.exercise: High exercise needs. Love swimming and need daily vigorous activity.

verdict.summary: Athletic, intelligent, hypoallergenic companions. PWDs love water and need lots of exercise. Great for active families, especially those who enjoy swimming and water activities.

description.overview: Pugs are charming, mischievous dogs with expressive faces. They were bred to be companions and excel at the job. They're known for their motto: 'multum in parvo' (much in little).
description.temperament: Pugs are playful, charming, and love attention. They're great with children and other pets. They live to love and be loved.
description.health: Brachycephalic breed prone to breathing issues, eye problems, skin infections, and overheating. Regular vet care important.
description.exercise: Low exercise needs. Short walks and indoor play. Avoid heat and strenuous activity.

verdict.summary: Lovable clowns that live to make you smile. Pugs are great family dogs but have health challenges. Best for apartment living and those who can handle potential breathing issues and vet bills.

description.overview: Rhodesian Ridgebacks are dignified, athletic dogs known for the distinctive ridge of hair along their backs. They were bred to hunt lions in Africa.
description.temperament: Ridgebacks are loyal, dignified, and protective. They're reserved with strangers but devoted to family. Need confident handling.
description.health: Generally healthy. Watch for hip/elbow dysplasia, dermoid sinus, and thyroid issues.
description.exercise: High exercise needs. Need daily vigorous activity. Athletic and enjoy running.

not_for: Those wanting a very social dog
verdict.summary: Dignified, loyal athletes that need experienced handling. Ridgebacks are devoted to family but reserved with strangers. Best for confident owners who can provide leadership and exercise.

description.overview: The Rottweiler is a robust working breed of great strength. A gentle playmate and protector within the family circle, they observe the outside world with a self-assured aloofness.
description.history: Descended from Roman drover dogs, Rottweilers were used to herd livestock and pull carts in the German town of Rottweil. They later became police, military, and guard dogs.
description.temperament: Rottweilers are calm, confident, and courageous. They're devoted to their family but can be aloof with strangers. Early socialization is essential.
description.health: Prone to hip and elbow dysplasia, heart conditions, and cancer. Regular vet checkups and buying from health-tested breeders is important.
description.grooming: Easy to groom. Weekly brushing. Moderate shedding with heavier periods twice a year.
description.exercise: Moderate to high exercise needs. Daily walks and mental stimulation. Excel at obedience, tracking, and carting.

not_for: Those who can't commit to training
verdict.summary: Rottweilers are powerful, loyal protectors. Best for experienced owners who can provide leadership, training, and socialization. Not ideal for first-time owners or apartments. A well-trained Rottie is a wonderful companion.

description.overview: Samoyeds are beautiful white dogs known for their 'Sammy smile' and friendly nature. Originally bred to herd reindeer and pull sleds in Siberia.
description.temperament: Sammies are friendly, gentle, and love everyone. They're great with children and other pets. Known for their constant smile.
description.health: Prone to hip dysplasia, eye problems, and cardiac issues. Regular health screening recommended.
description.exercise: High exercise needs. Need daily activity and mental stimulation. Love cold weather.

verdict.summary: Beautiful, friendly 'smiling' dogs that love everyone. Samoyeds need extensive grooming and exercise but reward you with endless affection. Best for cold climates and families.

description.overview: Shelties are small herding dogs that look like miniature Rough Collies. They're intelligent, eager to please, and excel at obedience and agility.
description.temperament: Shelties are bright and eager to please. They're sensitive and responsive to training. Can be reserved with strangers and vocal.
description.health: Prone to eye problems, hip dysplasia, and dermatomyositis. Sensitive to certain medications.
description.exercise: Moderate to high exercise needs. Excel at dog sports like agility and obedience.

verdict.summary: Brilliant, eager-to-please dogs that excel at training and dog sports. Shelties are loyal family companions but can be vocal. Heavy shedders who need regular grooming.

description.overview: Shiba Inus are ancient Japanese dogs known for their fox-like appearance and cat-like independence. They're bold, confident, and popular worldwide.
description.temperament: Shibas are independent and cat-like. They're loyal but not clingy. Can be aloof with strangers and don't always get along with other dogs.
description.health: Generally healthy. Watch for allergies, hip dysplasia, and eye problems.
description.exercise: Moderate to high exercise needs. Need daily activity but aren't as demanding as some breeds.

best_for: Those wanting a clean, cat-like dog
verdict.summary: Independent, fox-like dogs with cat-like personalities. Shibas are clean and charming but stubborn. Best for experienced owners who appreciate their independent nature. Not cuddly lap dogs.

description.overview: Shih Tzus were bred to be companion dogs for Chinese royalty. They're affectionate, outgoing, and want nothing more than to be with their people.
description.temperament: Shih Tzus are friendly and outgoing. They love everyone and make friends easily. They're playful but not hyperactive.
description.health: Prone to eye problems, breathing issues, hip dysplasia, and dental problems. Regular vet care important.
description.exercise: Low exercise needs. Short walks and indoor play. Sensitive to heat due to flat face.

verdict.summary: The ultimate lap dog. Shih Tzus are affectionate and adaptable, perfect for apartments and seniors. High grooming needs but minimal shedding. Avoid heat and rough handling.

description.overview: Siberian Huskies are beautiful, athletic dogs bred to run. They're friendly, outgoing, and mischievous. Known for their striking blue eyes and wolf-like appearance.
description.temperament: Huskies are pack dogs who love company. They're friendly with everyone, making them poor guard dogs. They're independent and can be stubborn.
description.health: Generally healthy but prone to eye problems and hip dysplasia. Need protection from heat.
description.exercise: Very high exercise needs—bred to run for miles. At least 2 hours daily. Escape artists—need secure fencing.

verdict.summary: Beautiful but challenging. Huskies need extensive exercise, secure fencing, and experienced handling. They're friendly and loving but independent. Best for active owners in cool climates.

description.overview: St. Bernards are massive, gentle giants famous for Alpine rescues. They're patient, loving dogs that adore children despite their imposing size.
description.temperament: Saints are patient, gentle, and wonderful with children. They're not aggressive but make good watchdogs due to size alone.
description.health: Prone to hip/elbow dysplasia, bloat, heart problems, and bone cancer. Shorter lifespan due to size.
description.exercise: Low to moderate exercise needs. Daily walks but avoid strenuous activity, especially while growing.

verdict.summary: Massive, gentle giants that adore children. St. Bernards are patient and loving but drool heavily and have shorter lifespans. Best for families with space who can handle the mess and costs.

description.overview: Vizslas are athletic Hungarian hunting dogs known as 'Velcro dogs' for their attachment to their owners. They're versatile hunters and loving companions.
description.temperament: Vizslas are extremely affectionate and attached to their families. They don't like being alone and thrive on companionship.
description.health: Generally healthy but prone to epilepsy, hip dysplasia, and eye conditions.
description.exercise: Very high exercise needs. Need vigorous daily activity. Excellent running and hiking partners.

not_for: First-time owners unprepared for high energy
verdict.summary: Affectionate athletes that want to be with you always. Vizslas need lots of exercise and don't like being alone. Perfect for active owners who want a constant companion.

description.overview: Weimaraners are elegant, athletic dogs with distinctive gray coats. Originally bred for hunting large game, they're now popular family companions for active owners.
description.temperament: Weimaraners are friendly, fearless, and energetic. They bond closely with their families and can suffer separation anxiety.
description.health: Prone to bloat, hip dysplasia, and gastric issues. Deep-chested breed—learn bloat symptoms.
description.exercise: Very high exercise needs. Need vigorous daily activity. Excellent hiking and running partners.

verdict.summary: Beautiful, athletic dogs that need extensive exercise. Weimaraners are loyal companions for active owners but can develop separation anxiety. Not for couch potatoes or those who work long hours.

description.overview: Westies are cheerful, sturdy little terriers with distinctive white coats. They're confident, entertaining companions with big personalities.
description.temperament: Westies are happy, confident, and entertaining. They have typical terrier independence and can be stubborn. Good watchdogs.
description.health: Prone to skin allergies, Legg-Calve-Perthes disease, and jaw problems. Regular grooming helps with skin health.
description.exercise: Moderate exercise needs. Daily walks and playtime. Enjoy activities but adaptable.

not_for: Those wanting a very obedient dog
verdict.summary: Cheerful, sturdy terriers with big personalities. Westies are hypoallergenic and apartment-friendly. They're independent and can be stubborn but reward you with endless entertainment.

description.overview: Yorkies pack a lot of personality into a tiny package. They're feisty, bossy, and full of terrier spirit—don't let their small size fool you.
description.history: Developed in Yorkshire, England during the 1800s by Scottish weavers who brought terriers south. Originally used to catch rats in textile mills.
description.temperament: Yorkies are affectionate with their owners but can be suspicious of strangers. They have big personalities and don't know they're small. Can be barky.
description.health: Prone to dental issues, hypoglycemia, luxating patella, and collapsed trachea. Regular dental care and careful handling important.
description.grooming: High maintenance coat requiring daily brushing and regular professional grooming. Many owners opt for shorter 'puppy cuts.'
description.exercise: Low to moderate exercise needs. Short walks and indoor play. Good for apartments but still need daily activity.

not_for: Those wanting a rugged outdoor dog
verdict.summary: Yorkies are feisty, portable companions perfect for city living. They need regular grooming but don't shed much. Best for adults without young children who enjoy pampering a tiny but spirited companion.
